subject: Computer Information
scope: Full syllabus Units 1–5 for law students (basic computing, MS Word/Excel/PowerPoint, AI ethics)
audience: Law students (BA-LLB / equivalent); introductory applied computing
bloom_objectives: [Understand] Explain computer system components, OS roles, internet basics, and digital communication in a legal-work context
[Understand] Summarize AI history, techniques at a conceptual level, and applications in the legal profession
[Apply] Prepare professional legal documents in MS Word (notices, agreements, petitions, affidavits) with formatting, citations, and track changes
[Apply] Organize legal records, case tracking, client data, and billing in MS Excel using formulas, filters, and charts
[Create] Design effective moot court / seminar presentations in MS PowerPoint using themes, SmartArt, charts, and purposeful motion
[Evaluate] Critique AI-assisted legal research and drafting for ethics, privacy, bias, and professional responsibility
[Apply] Complete syllabus practicals demonstrating responsible tool use across Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and AI
notes: Generated from official syllabus; subject-agnostic factory applied to law-computing course.
